name: VantaCode MBT to OWASP LLM Top 10

mappings.notes: Direct mapping. MBT prompt-injection covers both direct and indirect prompt injection as defined by OWASP LLM01.
Jailbreak vectors are a specific class of prompt injection focused on bypassing safety training.
Steganographic exfiltration through model outputs is a direct consequence of insecure output handling — outputs are not validated for hidden data.
Tool-use abuse occurs when model outputs are passed to tool execution without proper validation or sandboxing.
Direct mapping. MBT training-data-poisoning covers the same threat as OWASP LLM03.
Trojan models extend the training data poisoning concept to include pre-trained model artifacts with embedded backdoors.
Malicious ML pipeline dependencies are a direct instance of LLM supply chain vulnerabilities.
Compromised model registries represent supply chain compromise at the artifact hosting layer.
Unsigned model artifacts enable undetected supply chain tampering due to missing integrity verification.
Model provenance gaps weaken supply chain integrity by breaking the chain of custody from training to deployment.
Training data extraction is the primary mechanism for sensitive information disclosure from LLMs.
Context window data persistence leaks sensitive information across session boundaries.
Malicious agent skills/plugins directly map to the insecure plugin design category.
Misconfigured tool permissions grant agents excessive agency — more access than required for their intended function.
Agent-based lateral movement is a consequence of excessive agency — agents using granted access to move beyond their intended scope.
Tool-use abuse is excessive agency in action — the agent performs unauthorized operations through legitimately granted tool access.
Eval benchmark detection means models perform differently in testing vs production. Overreliance on benchmark results is a direct consequence.
Alignment faking compounds overreliance — users trust a model that appears aligned during evaluation but behaves differently when unmonitored.
Silent model drift degrades reliability without alerting operators, making overreliance on stale quality assumptions dangerous.
Model extraction via adversarial probing is the primary technique for model theft as described in LLM10.
Exposed training data and model weights directly enable sensitive information disclosure.
